headline: CBDT conducts mega outreach with theme ‘Bridging Borders, Building Trust’ at New Delhi, today
fullText:
CBDT conducts mega outreach with theme ‘Bridging Borders, Building Trust’ at New Delhi, today Outreach aimed to raise awareness among stakeholders of provisions of new Income-tax Act, 2025, the Income-tax Rules, 2026, and new forms, with an emphasis on International Taxation, Transfer Pricing, and Advance Pricing Agreements

The Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (International Taxation) North Zone organised a Mega Outreach Programme titled ‘Bridging Borders, Building Trust’ at New Delhi, today. The outreach aimed to raise awareness among stakeholders of the provisions of the new Income-tax Act, 2025, the Income-tax Rules, 2026, and the new forms, with an emphasis on International Taxation, Transfer Pricing, and Advance Pricing Agreements.

In his keynote address, Shri Prasenjit Singh, Member (Legislation), CBDT, highlighted the Government's efforts to simplify laws and rules and promote trust-based governance. He also discussed the decriminalisation of several provisions of the Income-tax Act, measures to ensure timely taxpayer services, and data-driven NUDGE campaigns to encourage voluntary compliance.

During the programme, Shri Prasenjit Singh also launched nine brochures on key aspects of International Taxation — including Safe Harbour, APA, Foreign Tax Credit, and TRC — to provide effective guidance to taxpayers. The Department’s AI-powered chatbot ‘Kar Sathi’ was also demonstrated.

This outreach is part of PRARAMBH initiative, the Department’s flagship stakeholder engagement series. About 150 participants from industry, MNCs, ICAI, tax professionals, and Department officials attended.

In her opening address earlier, Ms Monica Bhatia, Pr.CCIT (International Taxation) emphasised the importance of tax certainty, consistency, and credibility in a globalised economy. She announced upcoming global outreach programmes organised by the International Taxation Division, in collaboration with Indian Missions abroad.

Participants from various associations, ICAI, industry, and tax consultancy firms actively engaged and appreciated the Department’s outreach efforts. They posed numerous queries, which were answered by senior Department officials, including Joint Secretaries and Directors from the Tax Policy Division and Foreign Tax Division of the CBDT. The participants expressed appreciation for the responses.
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) held a mega‑outreach titled “Bridging Borders, Building Trust” in New Delhi to disseminate key provisions of the Income‑Tax Act, 2025, and Income‑Tax Rules, 2026, with special focus on international tax matters—transfer pricing, advance pricing agreements (APAs) and related doctrines. The event, part of the “PRARAMBH” stakeholder‑engagement series, drew 150 participants from industry, multinational corporations (MNCs), the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI), tax professionals and CBDT officials.

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- **Policy Parity:** Introduction of nine brochures (Safe Harbour, APA, Foreign Tax Credit, Transfer‑Rate Contracts (TRC), etc.).  
- **Technological Leverage:** Demonstration of the AI‑based chatbot “Kar Sathi” for quick query resolution.  
- **Decriminalisation & NUDGES:** Highlighted the removal of certain offenses and deployment of data‑driven “nudges” encouraging voluntary compliance.  
- **Educational Initiatives:** Concrete guidelines on international tax fundamentals and the impact of the 2025 Act on a globalised economy.  
- **Future Outlook:** Commitment to further global outreach programmes in collaboration with Indian missions abroad.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
By clarifying the new tax framework—especially in cross‑border contexts—CBDT aims to enhance tax certainty, reduce compliance costs, and build investor confidence. The decriminalisation reforms reduce litigation, while AI tools promote transparency and responsiveness. This holistic approach safeguards India’s position as a preferred destination for foreign direct investment.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **Current Affairs (IAS/IPS):** Recent policy updates, decriminalisation trends, and AI adoption in governance.  
- **Tax Policy & International Law:** Understanding transfer pricing, APAs, and treaties—key components of the “International Taxation” theme.  
- **Governance & Public Policy:** The push for trust‑based governance, “nudging” compliance, and stakeholder engagement aligns with the 2023 “Centre‑State” policy discourse.  
- **Economic & Development Issues:** Impact of tax certainty on FDI inflows and economic growth—central to questions on monetary policy and fiscal management.

headline: Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi shares glimpses from the Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh
fullText:
Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi shares glimpses from the Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today shared glimpses from the Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, emphasising the government's unwavering commitment to women's empowerment, the inspiring participation of Nari Shakti, and the continuous development of Kashi.

In a series of posts on X, the Prime Minister wrote:

"वाराणसी के महिला सम्मेलन में हमारी नारीशक्ति की सहभागिता अत्यंत प्रेरणादायक रही।"

वाराणसी के महिला सम्मेलन में हमारी नारीशक्ति की सहभागिता अत्यंत प्रेरणादायक रही। pic.twitter.com/HzJ1J8Fg88

"हमारी सरकार की नीतियों के केंद्र में बहनों-बेटियों को रखा गया है। काशी में महिला सम्मेलन का अवसर भी नारीशक्ति के वंदन और विकास का उत्सव है।"

हमारी सरकार की नीतियों के केंद्र में बहनों-बेटियों को रखा गया है। काशी में महिला सम्मेलन का अवसर भी नारीशक्ति के वंदन और विकास का उत्सव है। pic.twitter.com/50CCe34wK4

"उत्तर प्रदेश में बनास डेयरी से जुड़ी लाखों बहनें बहुत ही शानदार काम कर रही हैं। आज इन बहनों को सीधे उनके बैंक खातों में बोनस मिले हैं। मैं इन सभी बहनों को बहुत-बहुत बधाई देता हूं।"

उत्तर प्रदेश में बनास डेयरी से जुड़ी लाखों बहनें बहुत ही शानदार काम कर रही हैं। आज इन बहनों को सीधे उनके बैंक खातों में बोनस मिले हैं। मैं इन सभी बहनों को बहुत-बहुत बधाई देता हूं। pic.twitter.com/xQHGYUpUOf

"देश की बहनें-बेटियां महिला आरक्षण पर ब्रेक लगाने वाले परिवारवादी और तुष्टिकरण में डूबे दलों की कुटिल मंशा को पहचान गई हैं। असम, केरलम, पुडुचेरी, बंगाल और तमिलनाडु में रिकॉर्ड मतदान इन्हें सजा देने के लिए ही है।"

देश की बहनें-बेटियां महिला आरक्षण पर ब्रेक लगाने वाले परिवारवादी और तुष्टिकरण में डूबे दलों की कुटिल मंशा को पहचान गई हैं। असम, केरलम, पुडुचेरी, बंगाल और तमिलनाडु में रिकॉर्ड मतदान इन्हें सजा देने के लिए ही है। pic.twitter.com/gSCbNC684g

"कनेक्टिविटी हो, हेल्थकेयर इकोसिस्टम हो या फिर काशी की विरासत का संरक्षण, यहां के लोगों का जीवन आसान बनाने के लिए हम कोई कोर-कसर नहीं छोड़ रहे हैं।"

कनेक्टिविटी हो, हेल्थकेयर इकोसिस्टम हो या फिर काशी की विरासत का संरक्षण, यहां के लोगों का जीवन आसान बनाने के लिए हम कोई कोर-कसर नहीं छोड़ रहे हैं। pic.twitter.com/uJUJT4vser
summaryText:
Here’s a UPSC study note based on the provided article, structured as requested:

**Mahila Sammelan & Women’s Empowerment – UPSC Study Note**

**1. CONTEXT:**
Prime Minister Modi highlighted the Mahila Sammelan (Women’s Conference) in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, emphasizing the significant role of women in the region and the government’s focus on their empowerment. The event showcased initiatives like the Banas Dairy cooperative, a successful women-led enterprise. 

**2. KEY FEATURES:**
* **Nari Shakti Focus:** The core theme was celebrating and developing “Nari Shakti” (women’s power).
* **Banas Dairy:**  The successful direct benefit transfer (DBT) of bonuses to millions of women involved in the Banas Dairy cooperative demonstrated a key rural development initiative.
* **Women’s Political Participation:** The PM referenced record voter turnout in states resisting women’s reservation in Parliament, framing it as a rebuke to “family-oriented” and “appeasement-driven” parties.
* **Developmental Focus:**  Mention of connectivity improvements and healthcare infrastructure development in Varanasi underscored a broader commitment to regional development.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE:**
This event underscores the Modi government’s prioritization of women’s economic and political empowerment. The Banas Dairy example represents a successful model of rural entrepreneurship and women’s self-reliance. The reference to voter turnout highlights a strategic attempt to mobilize women voters and counter opposition narratives. 

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE:**
* **GS Paper 1:**  Women’s empowerment, rural development, social justice, and gender equality.
* **GS Paper 2:**  Government policies, economic development, social welfare schemes, and political dynamics.
* **Ethics Paper:**  Social responsibility, inclusive growth, and the role of women in nation-building. 
* **Potential Essay Topics:**  Analyzing the effectiveness of DBT schemes, the role of women in rural economies, or the impact of women’s political participation on governance.  


Would you like me to elaborate on any specific aspect of this note, or perhaps create a note on a related topic?

headline: CGST Delhi South Commissionerate detects fraudulent availment of Input Tax Credit (ITC) of around ₹60.59 crore through bogus invoices valued at ₹397.23 crore; one arrested
fullText:
CGST Delhi South Commissionerate detects fraudulent availment of Input Tax Credit (ITC) of around ₹60.59 crore through bogus invoices valued at ₹397.23 crore; one arrested

In another significant enforcement action against GST fraud, the Anti-Evasion Branch of CGST Delhi South Commissionerate has arrested the son of a director of the beneficiary entity in a major case involving fraudulent availment and utilisation of Input Tax Credit (ITC) of ₹60.59 crore through bogus invoices valued at ₹397.23 crore.

Investigation revealed that a non-functional entity was engaged in the fraudulent passing of inadmissible ITC without any actual supply of goods or services. Verification of its declared place of business established that the premises was merely a co-working space, with no evidence of genuine commercial activity, stock, invoices, or financial records. Further probe identified another entity engaged in smartphone trading as the principal beneficiary of the fraudulent scheme.

The son of the director of the beneficiary entity, who was actively managing and controlling its business operations and financial transactions, was found to have knowingly availed and utilised fake ITC through fraudulent transactions supported solely by paper invoices. Examination of statements, financial trails, and transactional records exposed a carefully orchestrated network designed exclusively to defraud government revenue.

The offence, involving deliberate contravention of Section 16 and punishable under Section 132 of the CGST Act, 2017, led to the arrest of the accused, who has been remanded to judicial custody for 14 days.

CGST Delhi South Commissionerate remains steadfast in its crackdown on tax evasion, ensuring strict compliance and safeguarding public revenue through robust enforcement action.

Read this release in: हिन्दी
summaryText:
**CONTEXT:**

The Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Delhi South Commissionerate has taken significant action against Goods and Services Tax (GST) fraud by detecting and arresting a tax evader. The case involves the detection of fraudulent availing and utilization of Input Tax Credit (ITC) worth ₹60.59 crore through the issuance of bogus invoices valued at ₹397.23 crore.

**KEY FEATURES:**

- **Fraudulent ITC availing**: A non-functional entity was engaged in passing inadmissible ITC without actual supply of goods or services.
- **Co-working space as business premises**: The entity's declared place of business turned out to be a co-working space with no signs of commercial activity, stock, invoices, or financial records.
- **Principal beneficiary entity**: Another smartphone trading entity was found to be the primary beneficiary of the fraudulent scheme.
- **Active management by accused**: The son of the director of the beneficiary entity actively managed and controlled its business operations and financial transactions.
- **Evidence of deliberate fraud**: A network of fake transactions was set up to defraud government revenue.
- **Offence punished**: The accused was arrested under Section 132 of the CGST Act, 2017, for deliberate contravention of Section 16.

**SIGNIFICANCE:**

- **Strict compliance and enforcement**: The CGST Delhi South Commissionerate is cracking down on tax evasion and ensuring safeguarding of public revenue through robust action.
- **Effective detection and prosecution**: The Commissionerate has effectively detected and prosecuted a significant GST fraud case, demonstrating its commitment to tax compliance.
- **Loss prevention**: The Commissionerate's actions have prevented a substantial loss of public revenue.

**UPSC RELEVANCE:**

- **Taxation and Revenue**: The case is relevant to the topic of taxation and revenue in the Indian Constitution and its implications on economic policy.
- **Economic Governance**: The case highlights the importance of effective governance and enforcement mechanisms to prevent tax evasion and ensure public revenue.
- **GST and its implications**: The case provides insight into the functioning and challenges of the GST regime in India, which is an essential aspect of UPSC examination.

headline: Raksha Mantri holds bilateral meetings with his Kyrgyz, Kazakh & Belarusian counterparts in Bishkek to further strengthen defence cooperation
fullText:
Raksha Mantri holds bilateral meetings with his Kyrgyz, Kazakh & Belarusian counterparts in Bishkek to further strengthen defence cooperation Discusses regional & global security with Chinese & Russian Defence Ministers in view of West Asia situationShri Rajnath Singh gifts two Bhishm Aarogya Maitri Health Cube to Kyrgyzstan for HADR & SAR operations

Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh held bilateral meetings with his Kyrgyz, Kazakh and Belarusian counterparts on the sidelines of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan on April 28, 2026. During the meeting with the Defence Minister of Kyrgyzstan Major General Mukambetov Ruslan Mustafaevich, both sides discussed ways to strengthen the existing bilateral defence cooperation.

Raksha Mantri gifted the two Bhishm Aarogya Maitri Health Cube to the Kyrgyz side, which have been indigenously developed in India to ensure Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief, and Search & Rescue during natural calamities

Shri Rajnath Singh also announced the completion of the project of provisioning of 12 sets of Computer Systems each at IT Centres of Kyrgyz Military Institute, Bishkek and Military Unit 36806- Osh, followed by the successful installation of Wargaming Software and On-the-Job Training of the Personnel of Kyrgyz Armed Forces.

In his meeting with the Minister of Defence of Kazakhstan, Raksha Mantri emphasised that defence cooperation between the two countries encompasses diverse sectors and it forms an important pillar of bilateral partnership.

During the meeting with the Minister of Defence of Belarus Lieutenant General Viktor Khrenin, Shri Rajnath Singh underlined that India attaches high priority to developing mutually beneficial partnership with Belarus and identified capacity building & training as among the major areas of cooperation.

In addition, Raksha Mantri briefly interacted with Defence Minister of China Admiral Dong Jun and Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov. During the discussions, the Ministers shared views on the regional and global security landscape in particular the developments in West Asia.

Earlier, Shri Rajnath Singh & other Defence Ministers paid a courtesy call on Kyrgyz President Mr Sadyr Zhaparov.

The Defence Ministers also laid wreaths at the Victory Square in Bishkek.
summaryText:
### 1. CONTEXT
The 2026 Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan, hosted Raksha Mantri Rajnath Singh’s bilateral and multilateral engagements, held amid rising West Asia security tensions to advance India’s defence diplomacy in the extended neighbourhood.

### 2. KEY FEATURES
Bilateral: (1) Kyrgyzstan: Gifted 2 indigenous Bhishm Aarogya Maitri Health Cubes for HADR/SAR; completed IT projects (12 computer sets each at 2 military facilities, wargaming software installation). (2) Kazakhstan: Defence cooperation flagged as core bilateral pillar. (3) Belarus: Priority partnership with focus on capacity building and training.
Multilateral: Discussed West Asia developments with Russian (Andrei Belousov) and Chinese (Dong Jun) counterparts; paid courtesy call on Kyrgyz President Zhaparov; laid wreaths at Victory Square.

### 3. SIGNIFICANCE
Advances India’s Connect Central Asia policy, expanding its defence footprint in a strategic, energy-rich region. Showcases Make in India indigenisation in dual-use humanitarian tech. Strengthens SCO’s mandate as a multipolar security dialogue platform, aligning with India’s multi-aligned foreign policy. Bolsters India’s profile as a net security provider for HADR operations.

### 4. UPSC RELEVANCE
Prelims: SCO member states, Bhishm Aarogya Maitri Health Cube features.
Mains: GS2 (India’s Central Asia policy, SCO’s role, defence diplomacy), GS3 (indigenous defence technology, HADR frameworks).

headline: All India Institute of Ayurveda–iCAINE signs MoU with Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University
fullText:
All India Institute of Ayurveda–iCAINE signs MoU with Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University AIIA–iCAINE–DSRRAU partnership to strengthen innovation ecosystem in Ayurveda education

The All India Institute of Ayurveda – Incubation Centre for Ayurveda Innovation and Entrepreneurship (AIIA–iCAINE), New Delhi, signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on 27 April 2026 with Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University, Jodhpur, to strengthen innovation, research, technological advancement and entrepreneurship in the Ayurveda sector.

The MoU was signed by Prof. (Vaidya) Pradeep Kumar Prajapati, Director, AIIA, on behalf of AIIA–iCAINE and Prof. (Vaidya) Govind Sahay Shukla, Vice-Chancellor, on behalf of DSRRAU. The signing ceremony was held at the DSRRAU campus in Jodhpur in the presence of senior faculty, researchers, post-graduate scholars and students.

Speaking on the occasion, Prof. (Vaidya) Pradeep Kumar Prajapati described the MoU as a significant step towards taking Ayurveda to new dimensions globally and catalysing the translation of traditional knowledge into evidence-based, market-ready innovations. He stated that AIIA–iCAINE remains committed to building a vibrant ecosystem for Ayurveda-led innovation and entrepreneurship across academic institutions in the country.

Prof. (Vaidya) Govind Sahay Shukla stated that the collaboration will open diverse opportunities for innovation and entrepreneurship for students, research scholars and faculty members of the University and further the institutional vision of “Blending Tradition with Innovation for Global Wellness”.

On the occasion, Shri Sujith Eranezhath, Chief Executive Officer, AIIA–iCAINE, addressed the gathering on promoting Ayurveda-based innovation and extending technical guidance to ventures emerging from academic institutions. He outlined the structured support available through the pre-incubation and incubation programmes of iCAINE.

Under the MoU, students, research scholars and faculty members of DSRRAU will be provided mentorship, advisory support, training, capacity-building inputs, innovation programmes, idea-pitching camps, entrepreneurship-oriented workshops and structured interactions with industry stakeholders. Joint ideation camps, innovation challenges and industry interactions will also be undertaken under the collaboration.

Signed for an initial period of three years, the agreement is expected to advance the objectives of the Ministry of Ayush relating to innovation, entrepreneurship and sectoral development, and create a supportive environment for Ayurveda-led ventures rooted in India’s traditional knowledge systems.

Read this release in: हिन्दी
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
On 27 April 2026 the All India Institute of Ayurveda – Incubation Centre for Ayurveda Innovation and Entrepreneurship (AIIA‑iCAINE) in New Delhi signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan Rajasthan Ayurved University (DSRRAU) in Jodhpur. The MoU aims to build a synergistic ecosystem that melds Ayurvedic tradition with contemporary research, technology and entrepreneurship. It follows central policies that treat Ayurveda as a “living science” and calls for translation of traditional wisdom into robust, market‑ready products.

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- **Three‑year partnership** focused on research, technology development and commercialization.  
- **Mentorship & incubation**: iCAINE will provide pre‑incubation, incubation programmes, training and capacity‑building for students, scholars and faculty.  
- **Innovation programmes**: joint ideation camps, innovation challenges, and industry interaction workshops.  
- **Entrepreneurship focus**: structured support for venture creation, idea‑pitching and funding guidance.  
- **Stakeholder engagement**: active interaction with industry partners to foster market linkages and scale‑up opportunities.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
The collaboration institutionalises Ayurveda‑centric R&D and catalyses the creation of a knowledge‑based value chain. It embodies the Ministry of AYUSH’s “infrastructure for innovation” agenda, providing a model for other traditional‑knowledge institutions. By fostering evidence‑based validation and commercial viability, the MoU addresses the gap between ancient insights and contemporary market demands, enhancing the global reputation of Ayurveda.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **Public Policy & Governance**: illustrates effective inter‑institutional cooperation, MoU frameworks and implementation monitoring.  
- **Science & Technology**: demonstrates application of research in traditional systems and the role of incubators in technology transfer.  
- **Economics & Entrepreneurship**: highlights how knowledge assets generate employment, innovation and rural development.  
- **Foreign Policy**: underlines Ayurveda’s export potential and “soft‑power” promotion.  
Understanding this case equips aspirants to discuss policy instruments, innovation ecosystems, and the integration of traditional knowledge into modern economic frameworks.

headline: CCI approves proposed acquisition by MAIF 4 Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd. of 42.5% equity share capital of Maple IM, 40% equity share capital of Maple PM and up to 37.5% of units of Maple Trust
fullText:
Competition Commission of India

CCI approves proposed acquisition by MAIF 4 Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd. of 42.5% equity share capital of Maple IM, 40% equity share capital of Maple PM and up to 37.5% of units of Maple Trust

The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has approved the proposed acquisition by MAIF 4 Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd. of 42.5% of the equity share capital of Maple IM, 40.0% of the equity share capital of Maple PM and up to 37.5% of the units of Maple Trust.

The proposed combination entails the acquisition by MAIF 4 Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd. (MAIF 4 India 2/Acquirer) of the following:

42.5% of the equity share capital of Maple Infra InvIT Investment Manager Private Limited (Maple IM) (IM Transaction);

40.0% of the equity share capital of Maple Highway Project Management Private Limited (Maple PM) (PM Transaction); and

up to 37.5% of the units of Maple Infrastructure Trust (Maple Trust) (Units Transaction).

(The IM Transaction, PM Transaction and Units Transaction are collectively referred to as the “Proposed Transaction”)

MAIF4 India 2 is a newly incorporated investment vehicle wholly owned by the Macquarie Asia-Pacific Infrastructure Fund 4, whose ultimate controlling person is Macquarie Group Limited.

Maple IM is the investment manager of Maple Trust.

Maple PM is the project manager of Maple Trust.

Maple Trust is a private trust settled under the Indian Trusts Act, 1882, and was registered as an infrastructure investment trust under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Infrastructure Investment Trusts) Regulations, 2014.

Maple Trust is, through its special purpose vehicles, engaged in the business of owning and operating road assets (through government concessions) in India.

Detailed order of the Commission will follow.

Read this release in: हिन्दी
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has cleared the acquisition of substantial stakes in three linked entities of the Maple infrastructure ecosystem by *MAIF 4 Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd.* – an arm of the Macquarie Asia‑Pacific Infrastructure Fund 4. The deal involves (i) 42.5 % of Maple Infra InvIT Investment Manager Pvt. Ltd. (Maple IM), (ii) 40 % of Maple Highway Project Management Pvt. Ltd. (Maple PM) and (iii) up to 37.5 % of the units of Maple Infrastructure Trust (Maple Trust).  Maple Trust, a SEBI‑registered infrastructure investment trust (InvIT), owns and operates toll‑road assets via special purpose vehicles (SPVs) under government concessions. The CCI approval follows its assessment that the transaction does not substantially lessen competition in the road‑infrastructure and infrastructure‑finance markets.

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
| Feature | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Acquirer | MAIF 4 Investments India 2 Pte. Ltd. – wholly owned by Macquarie Asia‑Pacific Infrastructure Fund 4 (Macquarie Group) |
| Target – Equity | 42.5 % in Maple IM (investment‑manager) + 40 % in Maple PM (project‑manager) |
| Target – Units | Up to 37.5 % of Maple Trust units (the InvIT) |
| Business | Ownership & operation of road‑asset SPVs under government concessions; investment‑management and project‑management services |
| Legal Structure | Maple Trust – private trust under the Indian Trusts Act, 1882; registered as InvIT under SEBI (2014) |
| Regulatory Clearance | CCI approval (no competition concerns); detailed order pending |

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
- **Sector consolidation** – Brings a global infrastructure fund (Macquarie) closer to operational control of Indian toll‑road assets, potentially improving efficiency and capital infusion.  
- **InvIT growth** – Signals confidence in the InvIT model as a vehicle for attracting foreign institutional investment into Indian infrastructure.  
- **Regulatory precedent** – CCI’s green light illustrates its willingness to allow sizeable cross‑ownership in infrastructure‑finance structures, provided competition is not impaired.  

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **Economic Development** – Illustrates how PPP models, InvITs and foreign fund participation are leveraged to bridge the infrastructure‑finance gap, a recurring GS‑II theme.  
- **Regulatory Framework** – Links competition law (CCI) with securities regulation (SEBI) and trust law, useful for papers on legal institutions and policy.  
- **Current Affairs** – The case can be cited in essays on “Infrastructure as a catalyst for growth” and “Foreign direct investment in strategic sectors.”  
- **Ethics & Governance** – Highlights the need for transparent approvals and the role of watchdog bodies in safeguarding fair competition.

headline: CCI approves acquisition of certain equity share capital of Avendus Capital Pvt Ltd. by Mizuho Securities Co. Ltd. on a fully diluted basis
fullText:
Competition Commission of India

CCI approves acquisition of certain equity share capital of Avendus Capital Pvt Ltd. by Mizuho Securities Co. Ltd. on a fully diluted basis

The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has approved the acquisition of certain equity share capital of Avendus Capital Private Ltd. by Mizuho Securities Co. Ltd. on a fully diluted basis.

The proposed combination relates to the acquisition of certain equity share capital of Avendus Capital Private Limited (ACPL) by Mizuho Securities Co., Ltd. (Mizuho Securities) on a fully diluted basis.

Mizuho Securities belongs to the Mizuho Financial Group, Japan, Mizuho Securities has worldwide presence with 20 offices globally and broadly operates in four business segments, namely: (a) global investment banking business; (b) global markets business; (c) research (in-depth investment information); and (d) retail and business banking.

Mizuho Securities is present in India, inter alia, through its subsidiary, Mizuho Securities India Private Limited (Mizuho India). Mizuho India was incorporated in 2010 and is registered with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) as a merchant banker.

ACPL is a SEBI-registered merchant banker. ACPL is present across four countries, i.e., India, Singapore, the United States of America and the United Kingdom, through its subsidiaries.

In India, ACPL offers mergers and acquisitions advisory, private equity syndication services and equity capital market solutions to clients across six sectors: (a) digital technology & consumer; (b) enterprise technology & services; (c) financial institutions group; (d) healthcare; (e) industrial; and (f) infrastructure.

Detailed order of the Commission will follow.
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
In 2024, the Competition Commission of India (CCI) cleared the transaction wherein Mizuho Securities Co., Ltd. (Mizuho) – part of Japan's Mizuho Financial Group – plans to acquire a majority stake in Avendus Capital Private Ltd. (ACPL) on a fully diluted basis.  Both entities are SEBI‑registered merchant bankers: Mizuho India operates as a global investment bank in India, while ACPL provides M&A, equity capital markets, and private‑equity syndication services across six strategic sectors.  

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- **Valuation & Dilution**: The acquisition reflects a fully diluted equity transfer, meaning all potential shares (options, warrants) are considered in the valuation.  
- **Sectoral Reach**: ACPL’s expertise spans digital, financial services, healthcare, industrials, and infrastructure, aligning with Mizuho’s growth strategy in emerging markets.  
- **Regulatory Approval**: The CCI’s mandate is to uphold competitive markets; its clearance indicates that market concentration concerns will not arise under Section 2(1)(e) of the Competition Act.  
- **Cross‑border Implication**: Mizuho’s global footprint combined with ACPL’s multinational presence broadens its footprint in the Asia‑Pacific region.  

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
- **Strategic Expansion**: For Mizuho, the deal consolidates its “one‑stop‑shop” vision for Indian capital markets, complementing its global banking segments.  
- **Competitive Landscape**: Elevated competition between India’s merchant‑banking houses could spur innovation, fee compression, and better client services.  
- **Market Consolidation**: Increase in high‑quality deal‑making entities elevates overall market efficiency and improves access to capital for high‑growth sectors.  

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **Economic Governance**: Illustrates CCI’s role in safeguarding market competition—essential for exam sections on Constitutional Law, Economic Regulation, & Public Administration.  
- **Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) Policy**: Highlights the interplay of foreign investment approvals with domestic competition policy.  
- **Financial Sector Evolution**: Offers insight into India’s financial liberalisation, merchant‑banking dynamics, and cross‑border capital flows – all critical themes in the Finance & Economics syllabus.  
- **Case‑study for Policy Analysis**: Serves as an exemplary case of merger assessment, valuation methodology, and regulatory compliance for the General Studies Paper‑III (Policy & Governance) and Paper‑IV (Governance & Administrative Technology).

headline: CCI approves proposed merger of A1 Agri Global, B.N. Agritech and Salasar Balaji Overseas Pvt Ltd. into BN Agrochem Ltd.
fullText:
Competition Commission of India

CCI approves proposed merger of A1 Agri Global, B.N. Agritech and Salasar Balaji Overseas Pvt Ltd. into BN Agrochem Ltd.

The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has approved the proposed merger of A1 Agri Global Limited, B.N. Agritech Limited and Salasar Balaji Overseas Pvt Ltd. into BN Agrochem Limited.

The proposed combination, inter alia, relates to the proposed merger of A1 Agri Global Limited (Agri), B.N. Agritech Limited (BNA) and Salasar Balaji Overseas Private Limited (Salasar) into BN Agrochem Limited (BNAC), which will be the only surviving entity, pursuant to Scheme of Amalgamation, which has been approved through board resolution 28th June 2025.

BNAC is involved in the procurement and trading of soyabean oil and palmolein oil.

BNA is involved in the procurement of crude edible oils (sunflower oil, palmolein oil and sunflower oil), followed by refining, repackaging and trading.

Salasar is involved in the procurement of edible oils (Kachchi Ghani Mustard Oil (KGMO), palmolein oil and soyabean oil), repackaging and trading.

Agri is involved in the sourcing, crushing, refining and trading of mustard seeds and mustard oil solely for captive consumption. It is also involved in trading of soyabean seeds, soyabean oil and palmolein oil.

Detailed order of the Commission will follow.

Read this release in: Gujarati
summaryText:
**UPSC Study Note – Competition Commission of India (CCI) Approval of BN Agrochem Ltd. Merger** --- ### 1. CONTEXT The Competition Commission of India, the statutory body that enforces the Competition Act, 2002, examined the proposed amalgamation of three edible‑oil players – **A1 Agri Global Ltd. (Agri), B.N. Agritech Ltd. (BNA) and Salasar Balaji Overseas Pvt. Ltd. (Salasar)** – into a single entity, **BN Agrochem Ltd. (BNAC)**. The Scheme of Amalgamation, approved by the boards of the three companies on 28 June 2025, sought to create one survivor (BNAC) that would operate across the value chain of soybean, mustard and palmolein oils – from procurement to refining, repackaging and trading. After a detailed market‑test, the CCI found no adverse impact on competition and gave its consent, subject to a formal order. --- ### 2. KEY FEATURES | Aspect | Details | |--------|---------| | **Sector** | Edible‑oil (soybean, mustard, palmolein) – a critical agri‑based industry. | | **Companies involved** | • **Agri** – sourcing & crushing of mustard seeds, captive mustard‑oil use; trades soybean‑seed/oil & palmolein.
• **BNA** – procures crude edible oils, refines, repackages, trades.
• **Salasar** – procures KGMO, palmolein & soybean oil, repackaging & trade. | | **Proposed entity** | **BN Agrochem Ltd.** – will become the sole survivor, integrating procurement, refining, and marketing functions. | | **Regulatory step** | CCI approval after assessing horizontal & vertical concentration, market shares, entry barriers and consumer welfare. | | **Outcome** | Merger cleared; detailed order to be issued later, possibly with standard behavioural/structural remedies (if any). | --- ### 3. SIGNIFICANCE * **Market efficiency** – Consolidation can lead to economies of scale, better price discovery, and reduced transaction costs in the fragmented edible‑oil market. * **Supply‑chain stability** – A single, larger player can ensure smoother procurement‑to‑distribution linkages, enhancing food‑security considerations. * **Competition vigilance** – The case underscores CCI’s role in monitoring concentration in essential commodities, balancing consolidation benefits against the risk of market dominance. * **Precedent** – Sets a reference point for future mergers in agro‑processing, especially where vertical integration (procurement + refining + trading) is involved. --- ### 4. UPSC RELEVANCE * **Economics & Governance** – Illustrates application of competition law, a key topic in GS‑II (Economics) and Public Policy. * **Agriculture & Food Processing** – Highlights structural changes in the edible‑oil sector, relevant for questions on agrarian reforms, agro‑industries, and FDI. * **Regulatory Institutions** – Demonstrates functioning of the CCI, useful for Polity‑Paper II (Institutions) and for linking with the role of other bodies like the Ministry of Corporate Affairs. * **Current Affairs** – Merger approvals are regularly featured in the “Mains Practice” and “Current Affairs” sections; remembering names (BN Agrochem, A1 Agri, B.N. Agritech) helps in answer‑writing and essay framing. --- *Prepared for UPSC aspirants – concise yet comprehensive for quick revision.*

headline: Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi participates in Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, inaugurates and lays the foundation stone of various development projects worth around Rs 6,350 crore
fullText:
Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi participates in Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, inaugurates and lays the foundation stone of various development projects worth around Rs 6,350 crore Our government is committed to ensuring women's participation in the country's policy-making: PMThe mission to build a developed India continues relentlessly; and when I talk about a Viksit Bharat its strongest pillar is India's Nari Shakti: PMAs the Member of Parliament from Kashi and as the Prime Minister of the nation, I seek your blessings to achieve a significant goal in the national interest and this major goal is implementing women's reservation in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies: PMOur government's policies have consistently accorded top priority to women's welfare: PMAlongside ensuring convenience and security, we have laid emphasis on enhancing the economic participation of women: PM

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, today addressed a massive gathering of women at a convention in Varanasi, where he inaugurated and laid foundation stones for development projects worth thousands of crores of rupees.

Describing the significance of the occasion, the Prime Minister noted that Kashi is the land of divine powers including Mother Shringar Gauri, Mother Annapurna, Mother Vishalakshi, Mother Sankatha, and Mother Ganga. The massive gathering of sisters and daughters had transformed the event into something truly sacred. "On this land of Kashi, I bow to all of you mothers, sisters, and daughters of Kashi," remarked Shri Modi.

Highlighting that the occasion celebrated both Nari Shakti and development, the Prime Minister noted that projects covering every type of development in Kashi had been inaugurated, along with works enhancing connectivity between Kashi and Ayodhya. Two Amrit Bharat trains, from Kashi to Pune and Ayodhya to Mumbai, were flagged off, providing modern connectivity options for people across Maharashtra to reach these holy cities. "This will further improve connectivity between UP and Maharashtra, giving people another modern option to reach Ayodhya Dham and Kashi Vishwanath Dham," stated Shri Modi.

Emphasising the critical role of women in nation-building, the Prime Minister described Nari Shakti as the strongest pillar of Viksit Bharat. He sought the blessings of the gathering for achieving a major national goal, implementing reservation for women in Lok Sabha and Vidhan Sabha. "I assure you that I will leave no stone unturned in ensuring your reservation right becomes reality," affirmed Shri Modi.

Underlining the transformative power of women's empowerment, the Prime Minister noted that when a woman is empowered in the household, the entire family gains strength, which in turn strengthens society and the country. Reflecting on the historical barriers faced by Indian women, the Prime Minister recalled the dismissive questions and commands that generations of girls have endured, questions about capability, necessity, and propriety. Acknowledging that such discrimination was not limited to Kashi but present throughout the entire nation, he noted that society had normalized this injustice. "These obstacles were simply accepted as the natural order, and this must change," asserted Shri Modi.

Recalling his commitment to breaking regressive perceptions, the Prime Minister shared that 25 years ago when he became Chief Minister of Gujarat, he launched two pioneering schemes for girls, Shala Praveshotsav to ensure more girls attend and complete school, and Mukhyamantri Kanya Kelavani Nidhi to help with their fees. "From then until today, women's welfare has been given the highest priority in our government's policies continuously," noted Shri Modi.

Detailing the comprehensive welfare initiatives since 2014, the Prime Minister highlighted that over 12 crore toilets were built, bank accounts were opened for more than 30 crore sisters, electricity connections were provided to more than 2.5 crore homes, and tap water reached more than 12 crore households. These initiatives placed sisters and daughters at the centre of national development, including schemes like Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, Mudra Yojana, Matru Vandana Yojana, and Ayushman Bharat. "Sisters and daughters were placed at the centre of every major scheme, reflecting our true commitment," noted Shri Modi.

Highlighting a successful campaign in Kashi, the Prime Minister recalled that two years ago, Sukanya Samriddhi accounts were opened for 27,000 girls in just one month, with Rs 300 transferred to each account. The scheme has strengthened girls' education while Mudra Yojana has ensured their earnings, and for the first time, crores of sisters have property registered in their names through PM Awas Yojana. "Today our mothers and sisters are truly becoming the owners of their homes," remarked Shri Modi.

Emphasising that convenience and security form the foundation of empowerment, the Prime Minister contrasted the current situation in UP with the past when it was difficult for girls to even step out of their homes. The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita has provided new confidence with quick decisions in crimes against women, while the network of women police stations and counseling centres continues to expand. "Anyone with wrong intentions toward daughters now knows very well what their fate will be," stated Shri Modi.

Underlining the importance of economic empowerment, the Prime Minister noted that when women's economic power increases, their voice in the household becomes equally strong. In the past 11 years, nearly 10 crore women have been connected to Self-Help Groups, including about 1.25 lakh sisters in Kashi, with lakhs of rupees in assistance enabling them to work independently. "Through such efforts, 3 crore sisters so far have become Lakhpati Didis, including thousands from Banaras," affirmed Shri Modi.

Highlighting the dairy sector's contribution to women's empowerment, the Prime Minister congratulated lakhs of sisters associated with Banas Dairy who received Rs 106 crore directly as bonus today. Encouraging women in Kashi's dairy sector to expand their work, he expressed confidence in continued growth. "This is just the beginning, Banaras will grow, Banas Dairy will grow, and this bonus will also keep increasing," affirmed Shri Modi.

Describing how the government is positioning women as change-makers, the Prime Minister highlighted efforts to make them self-reliant and entrust them with the leadership of campaigns to build a Viksit Bharat. He observed that Banking Sakhis are advancing digital payments, Insurance Sakhis are leading insurance initiatives, Krishi Sakhis are promoting natural farming, and Namo Drone Didis are spearheading the agricultural drone revolution. Furthermore, new avenues have opened up for daughters in the Army, Navy, Air Force, Sainik Schools, and defence academies. "Our government means women's empowerment, women's upliftment, and making women's lives easier," asserted Shri Modi.

Emphasising that women deserve greater roles in policy-making and national decision-making given their excellence across all fields, the Prime Minister noted that the idea of sisters' participation was a major reason behind building the new Parliament. The first work after the new Parliament was built was passing the Nari Shakti Vandan Act in 2023, providing 33 percent reservation for women after being stuck for 40 years. "Now it is necessary that this law be implemented as soon as possible," stated Shri Modi.

Expressing disappointment over recent parliamentary proceedings, the Prime Minister noted that a constitutional amendment was brought to enable more women to reach Vidhan Sabha and Parliament, but could not be passed. Expressing satisfaction, the Prime Minister pointed to record voting by women in Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Bengal, and Tamil Nadu.

Outlining the government's citizen-centric approach with the mantra of "Nagarik Devo Bhava," the Prime Minister emphasised that education, earnings, healthcare, irrigation, and grievance redressal remain the top priorities. He noted that with this sentiment, Kashi's development has been expanded today with multiple significant projects. "The signature bridge being built on the Ganga will strengthen Purvanchal's connectivity," observed Shri Modi.

Pointing out Kashi's emergence as a major health hub for North and Eastern India over the past decade, the Prime Minister stated that the 500-bed multi-super speciality hospital will further strengthen the healthcare ecosystem. Additionally, he mentioned that the foundation stone for a 100-bed critical care block has also been laid. "This will add a massive facility in Kashi for treating serious illnesses," observed Shri Modi.

Describing the comprehensive and sensitive nature of Kashi's development, the Prime Minister listed various initiatives including cleaning of the Ganga, development of ghats, construction of governance buildings, storage facilities for farmers in Harhua and Bhawanipur, old age homes, and women's hostels. "All these projects directly benefit Banaras's residents and reflect our commitment to sensitive, people-centric development," remarked Shri Modi.

Emphasising the continuous campaign to strengthen Kashi's heritage and legacy, the Prime Minister highlighted the development of Sant Kabir Sthali and restoration of Sant Ravidas Park in Nagwa as integral parts of this effort. "These are part of our ongoing campaign to preserve and strengthen our heritage," stated Shri Modi.

Concluding his address by drawing a parallel between Kashi's eternal nature and the ongoing development campaign, the Prime Minister expressed gratitude to the gathering for their blessings while extending best wishes for the development works being undertaken. "Our Kashi is imperishable and runs continuously, similarly, this campaign of development is also continuously dynamic," affirmed Shri Modi.

सुविधा और सुरक्षा का विश्वास देने के साथ-साथ... हमने बहनों की आर्थिक भागीदारी बढ़ाने पर बल दिया है: PM @narendramodi

summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi participated in a massive gathering of women at Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi, where he inaugurated and laid the foundation stone of various development projects worth around Rs 6,350 crore. The event celebrated both Nari Shakti (women's power) and development, underlining the significance of women in nation-building. The Prime Minister sought the blessings of the gathering for implementing women's reservation in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies, emphasizing that it is a major national goal. This event highlights the importance of women's empowerment and the government's commitment to ensuring their participation in policy-making.

**2. KEY FEATURES**

1. **Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi**: A massive gathering of women, where Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi inaugurated and laid the foundation stone of various development projects worth around Rs 6,350 crore.
2. **Women's Reservation**: The Prime Minister sought the blessings of the gathering for implementing women's reservation in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies, a major national goal.
3. **Development Projects**: The Prime Minister inaugurated and laid the foundation stone of various development projects, including two Amrit Bharat trains (Kashi to Pune and Ayodhya to Mumbai), enhancing connectivity between Kashi and Ayodhya.
4. **Women's Empowerment**: The Prime Minister emphasized the critical role of women in nation-building, describing Nari Shakti as the strongest pillar of Viksit Bharat (developed India).
5. **Comprehensive Welfare Initiatives**: The Prime Minister highlighted various initiatives since 2014, including the construction of 12 crore toilets, bank accounts for 30 crore sisters, electricity connections for 2.5 crore homes, and tap water reaching 12 crore households.
6. **Empowering Women**: The Prime Minister emphasized the importance of empowering women through schemes like Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, Mudra Yojana, Matru Vandana Yojana, and Ayushman Bharat.
7. **Economic Empowerment**: The Prime Minister noted that women's economic power increases when they are empowered, and highlighted the importance of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in empowering women.
8. **Recognition of Women's Contributions**: The Prime Minister congratulated lakhs of sisters associated with Banas Dairy who received Rs 106 crore directly as bonus.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**

1. **Emphasis on Women's Empowerment**: The event highlights the government's commitment to ensuring women's participation in policy-making and empowering them economically.
2. **Importance of Women's Reservation**: The Prime Minister's emphasis on implementing women's reservation in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies underscores its significance in promoting women's empowerment.
3. **Development Projects**: The inauguration of various development projects in Varanasi demonstrates the government's focus on infrastructure development and enhancing connectivity.
4. **Recognition of Women's Contributions**: The Prime Minister's recognition of women's contributions to the country's development underscores their importance in nation-building.
5. **Empowering Women through Schemes**: The Prime Minister's emphasis on initiatives like Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, Mudra Yojana, and Ayushman Bharat highlights their role in empowering women.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**

1. **Women's Empowerment**: The event highlights the government's commitment to ensuring women's participation in policy-making and empowering them economically, which is a crucial aspect of women's empowerment.
2. **Importance of Women's Reservation**: The Prime Minister's emphasis on implementing women's reservation in the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies underscore its significance in promoting women's empowerment, which is a relevant topic in the context of Indian politics and governance.
3. **Development Projects**: The inauguration of various development projects in Varanasi demonstrates the government's focus on infrastructure development and enhancing connectivity, which is a key aspect of urban planning and development.
4. **Recognition of Women's Contributions**: The Prime Minister's recognition of women's contributions to the country's development underscores their importance in nation-building, which is a critical aspect of social development and inclusion.
5. **Empowering Women through Schemes**: The Prime Minister's emphasis on initiatives like Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, Mudra Yojana, and Ayushman Bharat highlights their role in empowering women, which is a relevant topic in the context of inclusive development and social welfare.

In conclusion, the Mahila Sammelan in Varanasi highlights the government's commitment to ensuring women's participation in policy-making, empowering them economically, and promoting their contributions to the country's development. Understanding the significance of these initiatives and their relevance to UPSC syllabus is essential for aspirants to prepare for the civil services examination.

headline: MoPSW Convenes National Workshop with Coastal States to Drive Maritime Transformation under Amrit Kaal Vision 2047
fullText:
Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

MoPSW Convenes National Workshop with Coastal States to Drive Maritime Transformation under Amrit Kaal Vision 2047 Focus on Centre–State synergy, digital integration, and port-led growth to enhance India’s global maritime competitiveness

The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW) convened a National Consultative Workshop with Coastal States and Union Territories in New Delhi today, bringing together key maritime stakeholders to chart the next phase of India’s maritime growth under the Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047.

The workshop served as a collaborative platform to align national priorities with state-level initiatives, with a focus on accelerating port-led development, strengthening logistics efficiency, and advancing digital and sustainable maritime practices.

Delivering the keynote address, Secretary, MoPSW, Shri Vijay Kumar, IAS, underscored the significant transformation of India’s maritime sector in recent years, marked by improved port performance, enhanced global rankings, and growing integration with international trade networks. He noted that India is steadily positioning itself as a competitive maritime nation, supported by policy reforms, infrastructure expansion, and increased private sector participation.

Anchored in the Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047, the Government’s long-term roadmap envisages large-scale investments across ports, coastal shipping, inland waterways, shipbuilding, and green shipping. As highlighted in the background framework, the vision places strong emphasis on modernisation, digitisation, and sustainability as key drivers of growth.

Highlighting the scale of opportunity, the Secretary noted that India’s port capacity and operational capabilities are set to expand significantly over the coming decades, supported by ongoing investments, mechanisation, and port-led industrialisation initiatives. He also pointed to the Government’s focused push on shipbuilding as a strategic priority to enhance India’s position in the global maritime value chain.

A key focus of the discussions was the role of non-major ports (Other Than Major Ports), which handle nearly 46% of the country’s cargo. Recognising their critical contribution to regional development and coastal logistics, the Ministry emphasised the need for closer alignment of these ports with national frameworks, particularly in areas of process standardisation, digital integration, and operational efficiency.

The workshop deliberations were structured around three core themes: sectoral overview, regulatory and digital reforms, and state-level initiatives for capacity expansion and efficiency enhancement. Discussions underlined the progress made in infrastructure development, Public–Private Partnership frameworks, and ease of doing business measures, while also identifying areas for further improvement.

On the regulatory and technology front, participants discussed the implementation of the One Nation One Port Process (ONOP), aimed at standardising port procedures, and the Maritime Single Window (MSW) – Sagar Setu, which is expected to enable seamless, end-to-end digital transactions. The Indian Ports Act, 2025, along with other recent maritime legislations, was noted as a key enabler for strengthening Centre–State coordination and ensuring a transparent and future-ready regulatory ecosystem.

Coastal States and Union Territories shared their ongoing initiatives and plans, including infrastructure expansion, digitalisation, green port initiatives, and the development of shipbuilding and maritime industrial clusters. These presentations reflected strong alignment with national priorities and a shared commitment to building a resilient and efficient maritime ecosystem.

The workshop witnessed participation from Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, West Bengal, and the Union Territories of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu, Puducherry, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, and Lakshadweep. As detailed in the programme schedule, the deliberations enabled a comprehensive exchange of ideas and best practices across regions.

In his concluding remarks, Joint Secretary (Ports, IT & e-Governance), MoPSW, Shri Venkatesapathy S., emphasised that the discussions reflected a strong and shared commitment to advancing port-led growth, digitisation, and sustainability. He highlighted that initiatives such as the One Nation One Port Process, Maritime Single Window (Sagar Setu), and the Indian Ports Act, 2025, will be instrumental in driving efficiency, transparency, and integration across the maritime sector. He called for continued alignment of systems and policies across States and the Centre to translate these discussions into tangible outcomes.

The Ministry noted that the workshop marks an important step in strengthening cooperative federalism in the maritime domain. With sustained collaboration between the Centre and the States, India is well-positioned to unlock the full potential of its maritime sector and move decisively towards becoming a leading global maritime nation.

Read this release in: Gujarati , Urdu , हिन्दी
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT** The Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways (MoPSW) held a National Consultative Workshop in New Delhi, bringing together all 9 coastal states, union territories and key maritime stakeholders. The gathering was anchored in the *Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047* – the Government’s long‑term roadmap to make India a world‑class maritime nation. The workshop aimed to synchronise the Centre’s policy thrusts (large‑scale port, coastal‑shipping, inland‑waterway, ship‑building and green‑shipping investments) with state‑level programmes, and to firm up the “One Nation‑One Port Process (ONOP)” and the Maritime Single Window (MSW‑Sagar Setu) for digital, transparent operations. **2. KEY FEATURES** | Theme | Highlights | Implications | |-------|------------|--------------| | **Sectoral overview** | Port capacity to rise sharply; non‑major ports handle ~46 % of cargo. | Greater reliance on “Other‑Than‑Major Ports” (OTMP) for regional growth. | | **Regulatory & digital reforms** | – Indian Ports Act, 2025 (central‑state coordination).
– ONOP: uniform procedures across 13 major & OTMPs.
– MSW‑Sagar Setu: end‑to‑end e‑transactions. | Streamlined clearances, reduced dwell time, easier PPP participation. | | **State‑level initiatives** | • Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, AP, Odisha, WB, DNH, Puducherry, A&N Islands, Lakshadweep – infrastructure expansion, green‑port projects, ship‑building clusters. | Demonstrates cooperative federalism; creates “maritime industrial corridors”. | | **Sustainability drive** | Green‑shipping, renewable‑energy powered terminals, waste‑to‑energy pilots. | Aligns with India’s Climate Action Plan & IMO 2025 emissions targets. | **3. SIGNIFICANCE** - **Port‑led growth**: Boosts hinterland connectivity, lowers logistics costs, fuels Make‑in‑India manufacturing. - **Digital integration**: ONOP & MSW cut procedural lag, attract private capital, improve India’s global port rankings. - **Strategic ship‑building**: Reduces import dependence, positions India in defence & commercial ship value chains. - **Cooperative federalism**: Harmonises state plans with national vision, ensuring uniform standards and faster execution of mega‑projects. **4. UPSC RELEVANCE** - **GS‑II (Governance & Administration)** – Centre‑State coordination, PPP models, legislative reforms (Indian Ports Act 2025). - **GS‑III (Infrastructure & Environment)** – Port capacity expansion, green‑shipping, climate‑friendly maritime policies. - **GS‑I (Geography & Economy)** – Role of coastal states, OTMP contribution, maritime trade routes, impact on GDP. - **Essay / Ethics** – “Cooperative Federalism as a catalyst for sectoral transformation” – use workshop as a case‑study. *Key points to remember for prelims/ mains:* Vision 2047 → port‑led, digital, sustainable; ONOP & MSW as flagship reforms; non‑major ports = 46 % cargo; Indian Ports Act 2025 strengthens Centre‑State synergy.
category: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways
